title: Fair-Use Cache Keys, TTL, Clock Injection, Copies, and Eviction · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers fair-use cache keys, ttl, clock injection, copies, and eviction into a changed bounded TTL resource cache with observable decisions. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded TTL resource cache with observable decisions with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress…
objectives: Canonicalize endpoint, resource identity, and representation into a typed cache key without mixing user spelling or presentation.
Inject a clock, define freshness at exact boundaries, and distinguish fetched, stored, observed, and expires times.
Choose immutable values, deep copies, or documented ownership so callers cannot mutate cached history or other results.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Cache Key
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 4724 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile canonical cache decisions, freshness, ownership, and capacity eviction; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Cache Key.
